example.target: Naše društvo se brzo mijenja.
example.gloss: Our society is changing quickly.

example.target: Lokalna zajednica organizira pomoć starijim osobama.
example.gloss: The local community organizes help for elderly people.

example.target: Svaki građanin ima pravo na obrazovanje.
example.gloss: Every citizen has the right to education.

example.target: Ona je građanka Europske unije.
example.gloss: She is a citizen of the European Union.

example.target: Mlađa generacija drukčije gleda na svijet.
example.gloss: The younger generation sees the world differently.

words.note: adjective used as a plural noun (m. pl.)

example.target: Mladi danas teško nalaze posao.
example.gloss: Young people today have a hard time finding a job.

example.target: Starije osobe često žive same.
example.gloss: Elderly people often live alone.

example.target: U našem susjedstvu svi se poznaju.
example.gloss: In our neighborhood everyone knows each other.

example.target: Imam dobar odnos sa susjedima.
example.gloss: I have a good relationship with my neighbors.

example.target: Ravnopravnost žena i muškaraca još nije potpuna.
example.gloss: Equality between women and men is still not complete.

example.target: Nejednakost u društvu postaje sve veća.
example.gloss: Inequality in society is becoming greater and greater.

example.target: Svi ljudi trebaju imati jednake mogućnosti.
example.gloss: All people should have equal opportunities.

example.target: Ljudi imaju različita mišljenja o tome.
example.gloss: People have different opinions about that.

words.note: pravo na + acc.: pravo na rad

example.target: Svi imaju pravo na slobodu govora.
example.gloss: Everyone has the right to freedom of speech.

example.target: Novi zakon vrijedi od prvog siječnja.
example.gloss: The new law is valid from the first of January.

example.target: Svaka igra ima svoja pravila.
example.gloss: Every game has its own rules.

example.target: Ljudi na prosvjedu traže pravdu.
example.gloss: People at the protest are demanding justice.

example.target: Vlada je najavila nove mjere.
example.gloss: The government announced new measures.

example.target: Izbori se održavaju svake četiri godine.
example.gloss: Elections are held every four years.

example.target: Na izborima ću glasovati za promjene.
example.gloss: In the elections I will vote for changes.

example.target: Kandidat je dobio najviše glasova.
example.gloss: The candidate got the most votes.

example.target: Birači su jučer izašli na izbore u velikom broju.
example.gloss: Voters turned out for the elections in large numbers yesterday.

example.target: Koja je politička stranka pobijedila na izborima?
example.gloss: Which political party won the elections?

example.target: Političari obećavaju puno prije izbora.
example.gloss: Politicians promise a lot before elections.

example.target: Ne volim razgovarati o politici.
example.gloss: I don't like talking about politics.

example.target: Predsjednik je održao govor o krizi.
example.gloss: The president gave a speech about the crisis.

words.note: f. ministrica; fleeting a: gen. ministra

example.target: Ministar zdravstva govorio je na televiziji.
example.gloss: The minister of health spoke on television.

example.target: U demokraciji svatko ima pravo na svoje mišljenje.
example.gloss: In a democracy everyone has the right to their own opinion.

example.target: Grad je povećao proračun za kulturu.
example.gloss: The city increased the budget for culture.

example.target: Izborna kampanja počinje sljedeći mjesec.
example.gloss: The election campaign starts next month.

example.target: Za vrijeme krize mnogi su izgubili posao.
example.gloss: During the crisis many people lost their jobs.

example.target: Tisuće ljudi izašle su na prosvjed.
example.gloss: Thousands of people went out to the protest.

example.target: Učitelji su u štrajku već tjedan dana.
example.gloss: The teachers have been on strike for a week already.

example.target: Siromaštvo je velik problem u mnogim zemljama.
example.gloss: Poverty is a big problem in many countries.

example.target: Bogatstvo nije najvažnija stvar u životu.
example.gloss: Wealth is not the most important thing in life.

words.note: ASOO theme 'standard, ovisnosti'; ovisnost o + loc.

example.target: Ovisnost o mobitelu čest je problem kod mladih.
example.gloss: Addiction to mobile phones is a common problem among young people.

example.target: Teško je promijeniti loše navike.
example.gloss: It is hard to change bad habits.

example.target: Životni standard u gradu je viši nego na selu.
example.gloss: The standard of living in the city is higher than in the countryside.

example.target: Svatko ima potrebu za sigurnošću.
example.gloss: Everyone has a need for security.

example.target: U velikom gradu ima više mogućnosti za posao.
example.gloss: In a big city there are more opportunities for work.

example.target: Ljudi se često boje velikih promjena.
example.gloss: People are often afraid of big changes.

example.target: Nove tehnologije poboljšavaju kvalitetu života.
example.gloss: New technologies improve the quality of life.

words.note: impf. poboljšavati; poboljšati se = to get better

example.target: Vlada obećava da će poboljšati zdravstveni sustav.
example.gloss: The government promises to improve the healthcare system.

example.target: Turizam je važan za razvoj naše regije.
example.gloss: Tourism is important for the development of our region.

example.target: Nezaposlenost je ozbiljan problem u ovoj regiji.
example.gloss: Unemployment is a serious problem in this region.

example.target: Probleme treba rješavati razgovorom.
example.gloss: Problems should be solved through conversation.

example.target: Grad je konačno riješio problem parkiranja.
example.gloss: The city finally solved the parking problem.

example.target: Sudjelovala sam u humanitarnoj akciji.
example.gloss: I participated in a humanitarian campaign.

example.target: Radim kao volonter u udruzi za zaštitu životinja.
example.gloss: I work as a volunteer in an animal protection association.

example.target: Volonteri pomažu starijim osobama u kvartu.
example.gloss: Volunteers help elderly people in the neighborhood.

example.target: Sloboda govora temelj je demokracije.
example.gloss: Freedom of speech is the foundation of democracy.

example.target: Svi želimo živjeti u miru.
example.gloss: We all want to live in peace.

example.target: Mnogi su zbog rata napustili svoju zemlju.
example.gloss: Many people left their country because of the war.

example.target: Nasilje u školama ozbiljna je tema.
example.gloss: Violence in schools is a serious topic.

words.note: f. declension but can refer to men and women

example.target: Izbjeglice su dobile smještaj u našem gradu.
example.gloss: The refugees were given accommodation in our town.

example.target: U Hrvatskoj žive brojne nacionalne manjine.
example.gloss: Numerous national minorities live in Croatia.

example.target: Većina ljudi podupire tu ideju.
example.gloss: The majority of people support that idea.

example.target: Plaća ne smije ovisiti o spolu.
example.gloss: Salary must not depend on gender.

example.target: Diskriminacija na poslu zabranjena je zakonom.
example.gloss: Discrimination at work is forbidden by law.

example.target: Internet je postao dio naše svakodnevice.
example.gloss: The internet has become part of our everyday life.

example.target: Mediji imaju velik utjecaj na javnost.
example.gloss: The media have a big influence on the public.

example.target: Javnost još ne zna sve detalje.
example.gloss: The public does not yet know all the details.

example.target: Novinarka je postavila teško pitanje ministru.
example.gloss: The journalist asked the minister a difficult question.

example.target: Pročitao sam izvještaj o stanju na cestama.
example.gloss: I read the report on the state of the roads.

example.target: Naslov članka privukao mi je pažnju.
example.gloss: The headline of the article caught my attention.

example.target: Ta je vijest na naslovnici svih novina.
example.gloss: That news is on the front page of all the newspapers.

example.target: Jučer sam pročitala zanimljiv članak o zdravlju.
example.gloss: Yesterday I read an interesting article about health.

example.target: Uvijek prvo čitam sportsku rubriku.
example.gloss: I always read the sports section first.

example.target: Oglašavanje na internetu jeftinije je od televizijskog.
example.gloss: Advertising on the internet is cheaper than on television.

example.target: Na televiziji ima previše reklama.
example.gloss: There are too many commercials on television.

example.target: Publika je dugo pljeskala nakon emisije.
example.gloss: The audience applauded for a long time after the show.

example.target: Milijuni gledatelja pratili su utakmicu.
example.gloss: Millions of viewers followed the match.

example.target: Slušatelji mogu nazvati radio i postaviti pitanje.
example.gloss: Listeners can call the radio station and ask a question.

example.target: Časopis ima vjerne čitatelje.
example.gloss: The magazine has loyal readers.

example.target: Svako jutro slušam emisiju o kulturi.
example.gloss: Every morning I listen to a program about culture.

example.target: Gledamo novu seriju svaki petak navečer.
example.gloss: We watch the new series every Friday evening.

example.target: Nisam vidjela zadnju epizodu, nemoj mi ništa reći!
example.gloss: I haven't seen the last episode, don't tell me anything!

words.note: for a job interview use 'razgovor za posao'

example.target: Glumica je dala intervju za naš časopis.
example.gloss: The actress gave an interview for our magazine.

example.target: Njegova izjava izazvala je burne reakcije.
example.gloss: His statement caused heated reactions.

example.target: Ispod članka ima stotinu komentara.
example.gloss: There are a hundred comments below the article.

example.target: Ministar nije htio komentirati odluku.
example.gloss: The minister did not want to comment on the decision.

example.target: Njezina objava dobila je tisuću lajkova.
example.gloss: Her post got a thousand likes.

example.target: Mladi provode puno vremena na društvenim mrežama.
example.gloss: Young people spend a lot of time on social networks.

example.target: Obrisao je svoj profil na svim mrežama.
example.gloss: He deleted his profile on all networks.

example.target: Ona ima više od sto tisuća pratitelja.
example.gloss: She has more than a hundred thousand followers.

words.note: impf. only in this sense; pf. popratiti = to accompany with

example.target: Pratim vijesti svaki dan na mobitelu.
example.gloss: I follow the news every day on my phone.

example.target: Ne volim dijeliti privatne fotografije na internetu.
example.gloss: I don't like sharing private photos on the internet.

example.target: Podijelila je članak sa svojim prijateljima.
example.gloss: She shared the article with her friends.

example.target: Portal objavljuje vijesti svakih pet minuta.
example.gloss: The portal publishes news every five minutes.

example.target: Novine su objavile rezultate istraživanja.
example.gloss: The newspaper published the results of the survey.

example.target: Lažne vijesti šire se brže od istinitih.
example.gloss: Fake news spreads faster than true news.

example.target: Dobar novinar uvijek provjerava svoje izvore.
example.gloss: A good journalist always checks their sources.

example.target: Povjerenje u medije zadnjih godina pada.
example.gloss: Trust in the media has been falling in recent years.

example.target: Ne vjerujem svemu što pročitam na internetu.
example.gloss: I don't believe everything I read on the internet.

example.target: Mnogi su povjerovali toj lažnoj vijesti.
example.gloss: Many people believed that fake news.

example.target: Reklame imaju velik utjecaj na djecu.
example.gloss: Advertisements have a big influence on children.

example.target: Mediji utječu na mišljenje ljudi.
example.gloss: The media influence people's opinions.

words.note: countable in Croatian: jedna informacija, dvije informacije

example.target: Gdje mogu dobiti više informacija o tečaju?
example.gloss: Where can I get more information about the course?

words.note: impf. obavještavati; obavijestiti koga o čemu

example.target: Obavijestili su nas o promjeni termina.
example.gloss: They informed us about the change of date.

example.target: Radio nas obavještava o stanju u prometu.
example.gloss: The radio keeps us informed about the traffic situation.

example.target: Prije dijeljenja vijesti provjeri izvor.
example.gloss: Before sharing news, check the source.

example.target: Provjeravam e-poštu nekoliko puta dnevno.
example.gloss: I check my email several times a day.

words.note: fleeting a: gen. tiska; distinctly Croatian (vs 'štampa')

example.target: Ta priča je već tjednima u tisku.
example.gloss: That story has been in the press for weeks.

example.target: Gledali smo reportažu o životu na otocima.
example.gloss: We watched a feature story about life on the islands.

example.target: Urednik odlučuje što ide na naslovnicu.
example.gloss: The editor decides what goes on the front page.

example.target: Vijesti čitam na nekoliko portala.
example.gloss: I read the news on several portals.

example.target: Na kojem kanalu je utakmica večeras?
example.gloss: On which channel is the match tonight?

example.target: Gledali smo izravni prijenos koncerta.
example.gloss: We watched the live broadcast of the concert.

example.target: Emisija se prenosi uživo iz Zagreba.
example.gloss: The show is broadcast live from Zagreb.

example.target: Ovdje snimaju novu seriju.
example.gloss: They are filming a new series here.

example.target: Snimila sam kratki video za društvene mreže.
example.gloss: I recorded a short video for social media.

example.target: Snimka razgovora pojavila se na internetu.
example.gloss: A recording of the conversation appeared on the internet.

example.target: Ispred zgrade su postavili kamere.
example.gloss: They installed cameras in front of the building.

example.target: Otkazao sam pretplatu na taj časopis.
example.gloss: I cancelled my subscription to that magazine.

example.target: U slobodnim medijima nema mjesta za cenzuru.
example.gloss: In free media there is no place for censorship.

words.note: poznat po + loc.: poznat po dobrim intervjuima

example.target: Ona je poznata voditeljica na nacionalnoj televiziji.
example.gloss: She is a well-known presenter on national television.

example.target: Nakon te serije glumac je postao slavan.
example.gloss: After that series the actor became famous.

words.note: tržište rada = labor market; marketplace = tržnica

example.target: Na tržištu rada traže se programeri.
example.gloss: Programmers are in demand on the labor market.

words.note: pf. prijaviti se; prijavljivati se na natječaj / za posao

example.target: Već mjesecima se prijavljujem na razne natječaje.
example.gloss: I have been applying to various postings for months.

words.note: the usual word in Croatian job applications; cf. molba

example.target: U zamolbi objasnite zašto želite raditi kod nas.
example.gloss: In the cover letter, explain why you want to work with us.

example.target: Bivši šef napisao mi je odličnu preporuku.
example.gloss: My former boss wrote me an excellent recommendation.

example.target: Za ovaj posao nemam sve potrebne kvalifikacije.
example.gloss: I don't have all the necessary qualifications for this job.

words.note: SSS = srednja, VSS = visoka stručna sprema; standard on Croatian job ads

example.target: Traži se kandidat s visokom stručnom spremom.
example.gloss: A candidate with a university degree is sought.

example.target: Tvrtka nudi stalno usavršavanje zaposlenika.
example.gloss: The company offers continuous professional development for employees.

example.target: Radi kao pripravnik u odvjetničkom uredu.
example.gloss: He works as a trainee in a law office.

example.target: Probni rok traje tri mjeseca.
example.gloss: The probation period lasts three months.

example.target: Ne potpisuj ništa dok ne pročitaš uvjete.
example.gloss: Don't sign anything until you read the conditions.

example.target: Za rad nedjeljom dobivamo dodatak na plaću.
example.gloss: For working on Sundays we get a salary supplement.

words.note: very common in Croatian work culture; cf. regres (holiday bonus)

example.target: Ove godine svi radnici dobivaju božićnicu.
example.gloss: This year all workers are getting a Christmas bonus.

example.target: Kolika je satnica za ovaj posao?
example.gloss: What is the hourly rate for this job?

example.target: Neto plaća je iznos koji dobiješ na račun.
example.gloss: Net salary is the amount you receive in your account.

example.target: U oglasu je navedena bruto plaća.
example.gloss: The gross salary is stated in the ad.

example.target: Mjesečna primanja nisu mi dovoljna za život u Zagrebu.
example.gloss: My monthly income is not enough for living in Zagreb.

example.target: Za ovaj posao važan je timski rad.
example.gloss: Teamwork is important for this job.

example.target: Sutra držim prezentaciju pred cijelim timom.
example.gloss: Tomorrow I'm giving a presentation in front of the whole team.

example.target: Pregovori o plaći trajali su dva sata.
example.gloss: The salary negotiations lasted two hours.

example.target: Sindikat pregovara s vladom o plaćama.
example.gloss: The union is negotiating with the government about salaries.

words.note: tražiti povišicu = to ask for a raise

example.target: Nakon godinu dana zatražio sam povišicu.
example.gloss: After a year I asked for a raise.

example.target: U zadnjih godinu dana vidim velik napredak.
example.gloss: In the last year I see great progress.

words.note: raditi u struci = to work in one's field

example.target: Teško je naći posao u struci.
example.gloss: It is hard to find a job in one's own field.

example.target: Radi u malom obiteljskom poduzeću.
example.gloss: He works in a small family business.

example.target: Moja kolegica radi ovdje već deset godina.
example.gloss: My colleague has been working here for ten years already.

example.target: Svaki pripravnik dobiva svog mentora.
example.gloss: Every trainee gets their own mentor.

words.note: both a training session and a repair shop

example.target: Prijavila sam se na radionicu o pisanju životopisa.
example.gloss: I signed up for a workshop on CV writing.

words.note: poslovni put = business trip; poslovni sastanak = business meeting

example.target: Sljedeći tjedan idem na poslovni put u Njemačku.
example.gloss: Next week I'm going on a business trip to Germany.

example.target: Znanje stranih jezika velika je prednost.
example.gloss: Knowledge of foreign languages is a big advantage.

example.target: Rad od kuće ima prednosti i nedostatke.
example.gloss: Working from home has advantages and disadvantages.

example.target: Ljeti mnogi studenti rade sezonske poslove na moru.
example.gloss: In the summer many students work seasonal jobs at the seaside.

example.target: Ovo je odlična prilika za razvoj karijere.
example.gloss: This is an excellent opportunity for career development.

example.target: Ne boj se neuspjeha, iz njega se najviše uči.
example.gloss: Don't be afraid of failure, one learns the most from it.

words.note: isplatiti se = to pay off; uložiti trud = to put in effort

example.target: Njezin trud se na kraju isplatio.
example.gloss: Her effort paid off in the end.

example.target: Zadovoljni smo rezultatima ovog projekta.
example.gloss: We are satisfied with the results of this project.

example.target: Radimo na novom projektu s partnerima iz Austrije.
example.gloss: We are working on a new project with partners from Austria.

words.note: fleeting a: f. stalna; stalan posao = permanent job

example.target: Nakon dvije godine dobila je stalan posao.
example.gloss: After two years she got a permanent job.

example.target: Ovo je samo privremeno rješenje.
example.gloss: This is only a temporary solution.

words.note: fleeting a: f. odgovorna; odgovoran za + acc.

example.target: Tražimo odgovornu osobu za rad s djecom.
example.gloss: We are looking for a responsible person to work with children.

example.target: On je pouzdan kolega, uvijek završi posao na vrijeme.
example.gloss: He is a reliable colleague, he always finishes work on time.

words.note: fleeting a: f. samostalna; common in job ads

example.target: Kandidat mora biti samostalan u radu.
example.gloss: The candidate must be independent in their work.

example.target: Za rad s klijentima morate biti komunikativni.
example.gloss: For working with clients you must be communicative.

example.target: Tražimo motivirane ljude koji žele učiti.
example.gloss: We are looking for motivated people who want to learn.

example.target: Ambiciozni radnici brzo napreduju.
example.gloss: Ambitious workers advance quickly.

example.target: Poslodavac nudi fleksibilno radno vrijeme.
example.gloss: The employer offers flexible working hours.

example.target: Iskusni radnici pomažu novim kolegama.
example.gloss: Experienced workers help new colleagues.

words.note: vješt u + loc.: vješt u pregovorima

example.target: On je vrlo vješt u pregovorima.
example.gloss: He is very skilled in negotiations.

example.target: Marljivi studenti brzo nauče jezik.
example.gloss: Diligent students learn the language quickly.

example.target: Dobra tajnica mora biti organizirana.
example.gloss: A good secretary must be organized.

example.target: Tražimo kreativnu osobu za rad u marketingu.
example.gloss: We are looking for a creative person to work in marketing.

example.target: Njihova usluga bila je brza i profesionalna.
example.gloss: Their service was fast and professional.

example.target: Posao liječnika vrlo je stresan.
example.gloss: A doctor's job is very stressful.

example.target: Po mom mišljenju, mediji pretjeruju.
example.gloss: In my opinion, the media are exaggerating.

words.en: I consider that, I believe that

example.target: Smatram da svi trebaju imati jednaka prava.
example.gloss: I believe that everyone should have equal rights.

example.target: Čini mi se da ova vijest nije točna.
example.gloss: It seems to me that this news is not accurate.

example.target: Uvjeren sam da će se situacija poboljšati.
example.gloss: I am convinced that the situation will improve.

words.note: slagati se s + instr.: slažem se s tobom

example.target: Slažem se s tvojim prijedlogom.
example.gloss: I agree with your proposal.

example.target: Ne slažem se s tom odlukom vlade.
example.gloss: I disagree with that government decision.

words.note: biti u pravu; formal: u pravu ste

example.target: U pravu si, trebali smo ranije krenuti.
example.gloss: You're right, we should have left earlier.

example.target: Nemaš pravo, to se dogodilo prošle godine.
example.gloss: You're wrong, that happened last year.

words.note: s moje točke gledišta = from my point of view

example.target: Pokušaj vidjeti problem s moje točke gledišta.
example.gloss: Try to see the problem from my point of view.

example.target: Svatko ima pravo na svoje mišljenje.
example.gloss: Everyone has the right to their own opinion.

words.en: to consider, to be of the opinion
words.note: impf. only; smatrati da / smatrati koga čime

example.target: Mnogi smatraju da je zakon nepravedan.
example.gloss: Many consider the law to be unjust.

example.target: Svjedok tvrdi da je sve vidio.
example.gloss: The witness claims that he saw everything.

example.target: Njegova tvrdnja nije potkrijepljena dokazima.
example.gloss: His claim is not supported by evidence.

example.target: Imate li dokaz za tu tvrdnju?
example.gloss: Do you have proof for that claim?

example.target: Znanstvenici su dokazali da je teorija točna.
example.gloss: Scientists proved that the theory is correct.

example.target: Ne moraš mi ništa dokazivati, vjerujem ti.
example.gloss: You don't have to prove anything to me, I believe you.

words.note: a reasoned point, not a quarrel (= svađa)

example.target: Njezini argumenti bili su vrlo uvjerljivi.
example.gloss: Her arguments were very convincing.

example.target: Koji je razlog tvoje odluke?
example.gloss: What is the reason for your decision?

example.target: Najveća prednost života na selu je mir.
example.gloss: The biggest advantage of living in the countryside is the peace.

example.target: Svaki plan ima prednosti i nedostatke.
example.gloss: Every plan has advantages and disadvantages.

example.target: Nemoj se stalno uspoređivati s drugima.
example.gloss: Don't constantly compare yourself with others.

example.target: U usporedbi s prošlom godinom, cijene su više.
example.gloss: In comparison with last year, prices are higher.

example.target: Iz razgovora sam zaključila da nije zainteresiran.
example.gloss: From the conversation I concluded that he is not interested.

example.target: Ne treba zaključivati na temelju jednog primjera.
example.gloss: One shouldn't draw conclusions based on a single example.

example.target: Došli smo do istog zaključka.
example.gloss: We came to the same conclusion.

example.target: Predložila sam kolegama novo rješenje.
example.gloss: I proposed a new solution to my colleagues.

example.target: Stranke predlažu različita rješenja za krizu.
example.gloss: The parties are proposing different solutions for the crisis.

example.target: Imam prijedlog: idemo vlakom umjesto autom.
example.gloss: I have a suggestion: let's go by train instead of by car.

example.target: Nakon predavanja slijedi rasprava.
example.gloss: After the lecture there follows a discussion.

example.target: Cijelu večer raspravljali smo o politici.
example.gloss: We discussed politics the whole evening.

words.note: impf. uvjeravati; uvjeriti koga u + acc.

example.target: Uvjerila me da je to dobra ideja.
example.gloss: She convinced me that it is a good idea.

words.en: to try to convince, to assure

example.target: Prodavač me uvjeravao da je auto u odličnom stanju.
example.gloss: The salesman kept assuring me that the car was in excellent condition.

example.target: Sumnjam da će doći na vrijeme.
example.gloss: I doubt that he will come on time.

example.target: Nemam nikakve sumnje u njezino poštenje.
example.gloss: I have no doubt about her honesty.

example.target: Ovo je, bez sumnje, najbolji film godine.
example.gloss: This is, without a doubt, the best film of the year.

example.target: Reci mi istinu, što se dogodilo?
example.gloss: Tell me the truth, what happened?

words.note: f. gender: jedna laž, gen. laži

example.target: To je obična laž, ne vjeruj mu.
example.gloss: That is a plain lie, don't believe him.

example.target: Nemoj mi lagati, vidjela sam te tamo.
example.gloss: Don't lie to me, I saw you there.

words.en: to make mistakes, to be wrong

example.target: Svi ponekad griješimo.
example.gloss: We all make mistakes sometimes.

example.target: Pogriješila sam, oprosti mi.
example.gloss: I made a mistake, forgive me.

example.target: Nazvao sam pogrešan broj.
example.gloss: I called the wrong number.

example.target: Želim izraziti svoje neslaganje s tim planom.
example.gloss: I want to express my disagreement with that plan.

example.target: Djeca teško izražavaju svoje osjećaje riječima.
example.gloss: Children have difficulty expressing their feelings in words.

example.target: Ministar je naglasio važnost obrazovanja.
example.gloss: The minister emphasized the importance of education.

example.target: Profesorica stalno naglašava da treba vježbati padeže.
example.gloss: The professor constantly emphasizes that one should practice the cases.

example.target: Možete li mi dati konkretan primjer?
example.gloss: Can you give me a concrete example?

example.target: Činjenica je da mladi sve manje čitaju knjige.
example.gloss: It is a fact that young people read books less and less.

words.note: fleeting a: f. točna; distinctly Croatian (vs 'tačan')

example.target: Vaš odgovor je točan, čestitam!
example.gloss: Your answer is correct, congratulations!

words.note: fleeting a: f. sigurna; siguran u + acc.

example.target: Jesi li siguran da je to istina?
example.gloss: Are you sure that it is true?

example.target: Očito je da se nešto promijenilo.
example.gloss: It is obvious that something has changed.

words.note: with genitive: protiv rata; biti za ili protiv

example.target: Jesi li za novi zakon ili protiv njega?
example.gloss: Are you for the new law or against it?

example.target: Film je dobio odlične kritike.
example.gloss: The film received excellent reviews.

example.target: Lako je kritizirati, teže je nešto napraviti.
example.gloss: It is easy to criticize, it is harder to actually do something.

words.note: pf. pohvaliti; hvaliti se = to boast

example.target: Svi hvale novi restoran u centru.
example.gloss: Everyone is praising the new restaurant in the center.

example.target: Šefica me pohvalila pred cijelim timom.
example.gloss: The boss praised me in front of the whole team.

example.target: Trebam tvoj savjet oko posla.
example.gloss: I need your advice about a job.

words.note: donijeti odluku = to make a decision

example.target: To je bila najteža odluka u mom životu.
example.gloss: That was the hardest decision of my life.

words.note: impf. odlučivati; odlučiti se za + acc.

example.target: Odlučili smo se preseliti u Rijeku.
example.gloss: We decided to move to Rijeka.

example.target: O tome odlučuje gradsko vijeće.
example.gloss: The city council decides about that.

example.target: Kakav je tvoj stav o radu od kuće?
example.gloss: What is your attitude towards working from home?

words.note: ASOO theme 'Vrijednosti, sposobnosti, interesi'; sg. vrijednost

example.target: Zdravlje i obitelj moje su najvažnije životne vrijednosti.
example.gloss: Health and family are my most important life values.

example.target: Moj cilj je naučiti hrvatski do ljeta.
example.gloss: My goal is to learn Croatian by summer.

words.note: koja je svrha…? = what is the point of…?

example.target: Koja je svrha ovog sastanka?
example.gloss: What is the purpose of this meeting?

words.note: gen. smisla; imati smisla = to make sense

example.target: To nema nikakvog smisla.
example.gloss: That doesn't make any sense.
